enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs about a low-energy day in Boulder spent reorganizing lists in the wrong order and having to redo the work. He mentions recording 'History of Freedom', pulling a video he'd uploaded about an Asperger's diagnosis because it disclosed too much about others, and upcoming surgery the next day. He also talks about plans to finish uploading content to a website or 'box' and experimenting with text-to-speech at double speed.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Better to focus on one thing at a time and finish it completely
Had to delete it because it disclosed too much about other people; will edit it before re-uploading
Believes he can absorb spoken content at roughly double speed, which is about his natural speaking pace

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder, V-Log.
I was working all day long, basically trying to fix old stuff that I screwed up on these lists.
I put them in the wrong order, and I did the whole thing in the wrong order.
So I was just fixing it, dragging and dropping it all day long.
And that's all I did. I was listening to The History of Freedom today,
And recording it with my computer, playing it with my speaker and recording it with my computer.
And so that's kind of my focus.
Yesterday I uploaded, last night I uploaded a video about the Asperger diagnosis,
But then I had to delete the video because I disclosed too much about other stuff.
So I was going to splice a couple of bits out of that and then upload it again,
But I was too lazy to do that, so I'll do that tomorrow.
Yeah, it's complicated when you're doing videos when you mention other people.
And so, yeah, I was kind of tired today.
Today was a day where I'm tired, and I don't know why.
I slept in really late today, until like 12.
I was up late last night, but not so late that I should get up at 12.
I think I got more than eight hours of sleep last night.
I keep forgetting to write.
And then I had to have a nap today.
The new weed that I have is super strong.
I have a boost banner to cross between super strong and other ones.
So, yeah, I have to go into my surgery tomorrow.
It snowed all day today.
That was cool.
I have to go into my soul surgery tomorrow, and it's 11.82 now, so I have to get up at like 8 or 7.
So I'm going to go to sleep here pretty soon, go into surgery, and then come back,
And I'm going to, yeah, work on this list again, just trying to finish it.
I should be done by now, but I did it wrong.
I did it in the other order, and I thought all the things were ordered differently,
And I thought I was sure that that's how it was without even looking at the other ones.
It was stupid.
And then when I was done, I looked to make sure, and it was like, oh, no, it was all different.
I had to change everything.
And I could see why it was all different.
So that's the problem.
That's the problem.
You have to switch around and do one thing, and then switch it up.
You have to do something else because you're not as good at it anymore.
It's better to do one thing and just stay on that while you can be efficient with it.
So, yeah, so for the next two weeks, I'm going to work on that,
Just everything having to do with my website and my bicycle,
So I'll be as ready as I can.
I'm more ready, you know, when I start writing on my rollers.
And then that's when I'll start doing that half and half,
Half of that and then half the Google Earth thing.
And, yeah, I'll probably start doing some new things.
But, yeah, that's all I did today.
Maybe the same old, same old.
So, yeah, I'm excited when I get done with this book.
I think I'm going to be done with it tomorrow.
It's a big book.
It's like 1,000 pages.
But when I get done with it tomorrow, I'm going to put it up on the Google Box.
It'll be my first upload to Google Box.
I'm super excited.
And then just start doing a lot of them, you know,
And then I can like one by one knock them off
So that my whole list on my website is all MP3 Google Box and no Kindle at all.
So that's going to be groovy.
And then maybe after that, you know, when I have time,
I'll go on other sources and see if there's other books available.

Than how normal people read.
Even the best of the normal readers
Because Kindle reads fast.
At double speed, you can get through, well, twice as fast.
And it's about the same speed that I talk normally, you know.
It's about as fast as I'm talking right now, you know.
